posts.caption:
The women’s world number one @ArynaSabalenka understands that tennis can be opera: full of triumph and despair, grace and slapstick, sin and atonement. The 28-year-old has been at the top of her sport for the last year and a half, with a deep bench of brand associations—including with Gucci, Nike, and Audemars Piguet—to show for it. But since her earliest days on the tour, she’s been a fiery on-court presence, known for her outbursts (she’s a world-class racket-smasher) and tense exchanges with officials. (She attributes much of this to being “very much a Taurus”).
Time and training have helped her to temper that fire—but it also has its uses. “This is part of my personality that can drive me crazy, but it can also drive me into that real fight mode and help me play with passion,” Sabalenka says. “It’s two sides of the medal.”
For Vogue’s May 2026 digital cover, @robertjhaskell meets Aryna Sabalenka in Rome to talk winning, losing, grieving, learning, and never letting up.
Photographed by @stevenkleinstudio,Written by @robertjhaskell, Styled by @tonnegood, Hair by @sandyhullett, Makeup by @markcarrasquillo, Manicure by @nailsbyetsuko, Tailoring by @sara.on.set.tailor, Produced by @thatoneproduction, Shot on Location at @faenamiamibeach.  Vogue, May digital cover 2026.

posts.caption: Summer has officially kicked off in New York, and with it comes the beginning of the 2026 CFDA/Vogue Fashion Fund. This year’s 10 finalists, selected yesterday at CFDA headquarters, are: Aisling Camps, Amir Taghi, Terrence Zhou of Bad Binch TongTong, Emily Dawn Long, Julia Ferentinos of Juju Vera, Jamie Haller, Zane Li of Lii, George Inaki of Milamore, Claire Sullivan of Miss Claire Sullivan, and Zoe Gustavia Anna Whalen. Uniquely, this year’s designers are all womenswear or gender inclusive, with the exception of two jewelry brands; however, all represent New York’s bustling creative scene. Learn more at the link in bio.
posts.alt_text: There are fashion founders, and then there is @JJMartinMilan. The @LaDoubleJ founder and creative director joined @NicolePhelps for this Tuesday’s episode of #TheRunThrough to discuss the opening of The Lighthouse, her new five-story Upper East Side flagship, which houses not only fashion and homewares but also a dedicated “Light Temple” for meditation, sound healing, and community gatherings. Listen to the full episode at the link in bio.

posts.caption: A few others: winter 1998 Jeremy Scott with asymmetric heeled shoes to mimic Monroe’s walk; the winter 1995 Atelier Versace show, based on Monroe’s ‘Happy Birthday Mr President’ dress; Vivienne Westwood winter 1987, with hair based on Monroe as seen on Sarah Stockbridge. posts.caption: The best work bags for women should always meet two criteria: function and style. When searching for your own, ask yourself, is it durable? Will it be sizable enough to fit all of your essentials—laptop, notebooks, planners, wallet, keys, and beauty staples? Is it lightweight and comfortable enough to wear over your shoulders (and coat!), especially during a daily commute? At the link in our bio, shop the best work bags for women that will carry all of your essentials, no matter where you’re headed. Photographed by @jancrlo

✨ Creator, Actor, PHENOMENON! ✨ On the June-July cover of Wedding Vows, we present the trailblazing @mostlysane, Prajakta Koli!
From building a YouTube empire to being the lead for Netflix Original Mismatched, from being part of Michelle Obama’s Day Time Emmy winning docu-series to being UNDP India's First Youth Climate Champion, her journey is nothing short of extraordinary.
With her debut romance novel set to launch this year, along with her first Amazon Prime Original Andhera, Prajakta’s journey is a testament to the power of dreams, hard work, and staying true to oneself. She continues to inspire millions with her authenticity, relatability, and unstoppable spirit.

posts.caption:
𝐇𝐚𝐩𝐩𝐲 𝐄𝐯𝐞𝐧𝐭 𝐌𝐚𝐧𝐚𝐠𝐞𝐫𝐬 𝐃𝐚𝐲.
To the people who have attended 1,000 weddings...
not to sit in the crowd, but to orchestrate the magic.
To the people who know every family’s story...
but still calm the room and say,
“𝐷𝑜𝑛’𝑡 𝑤𝑜𝑟𝑟𝑦, 𝑤𝑒’𝑙𝑙 ℎ𝑎𝑛𝑑𝑙𝑒 𝑖𝑡.”
To the people who survive on coffee, adrenaline, and impossible deadlines.
To the people who gracefully handle,
“𝐽𝑢𝑠𝑡 𝑜𝑛𝑒 𝑠𝑚𝑎𝑙𝑙 𝑐ℎ𝑎𝑛𝑔𝑒..?”
when everything is already beautifully set,
yet still make it happen flawlessly.
To the people who miss meals,
miss sleep,
and miss their own celebrations...
just so someone else’s special day becomes unforgettable.
You are not just event managers.
You are therapists.
Dream builders.
And somehow...
you do all of that with absolute grace.
Today, the spotlight belongs to the people who spend their lives making sure everyone else’s shine.
𝑯𝒂𝒑𝒑𝒚 𝑬𝒗𝒆𝒏𝒕 𝑴𝒂𝒏𝒂𝒈𝒆𝒓𝒔 𝑫𝒂𝒚.
Now answer honestly...
How many times have you looked a client in the eye, said “𝐸𝑣𝑒𝑟𝑦𝑡ℎ𝑖𝑛𝑔 𝑖𝑠 𝑢𝑛𝑑𝑒𝑟 𝑐𝑜𝑛𝑡𝑟𝑜𝑙,” while you were still figuring things out, only to deliver absolute perfection at the end? 😅
#wvconnect #eventmanagersday

posts.caption:
@camimendes is having one of the biggest years in her career so far. A major summer blockbuster, the second feature film from her production company, and wedding planning are all on the roster for 2026. This month, Mendes steps into the sprawling world of Masters of the Universe as the sword-slinging Teela alongside Nicholas Galitzine, Idris Elba, and Jared Leto.
The project is a stretch from her days as Veronica Lodge in #Riverdale, which catapulted her into mainstream recognition nearly a decade ago. Mendes makes movies for “the girls and the gays,” she states lovingly, but her star power is only going to get bigger and brighter with Masters. “I’ve never been a part of something of this scale,” she says of the project.
In our Now Playing Issue, presented by @bvlgari, we chat with the actress about reinventing herself in this era of her life. Read it all in the full interview at the link in our bio.

posts.caption:
There would be no Pope Heyward without @jonathandavissofficial. Much like his smart, careful, deeply moral Outer Banks character, Daviss knows what it feels like to carry the pressure of proving your worth and refusing to let yourself or anyone else down. “What really stood out to me about Pope in season 1 was this idea of expectations and pressure that gets put on a lot of young Black teens and teens in general,” he says. “There’s this pressure to succeed, and it was something I truly have been through.”
Daviss still thinks back to his early audition days before landing the life-changing role on OBX, when the constant need to prove himself as a young actor felt emotionally exhausting. His mindset then? “You only lose when you quit.”
Head to the link in our bio for the full download in our Now Playing Issue presented by @bvlgari, from what to expect from the fifth and final season of OBX to his upcoming portrayal of a young Snoop Dogg.

posts.caption:
@victoriamonet is trading her claws for butterfly wings. The three-time Grammy winner is the definition of a multi-hyphenate: a culinary school graduate, a mother, and, of course, a singer-songwriter with a nearly two-decade-spanning catalog to her name. Now, using her 37th birthday as a launching pad, Monét has cleared her mental space and scrubbed her socials to prepare for her next chapter—an album she’s dubbed her very own Thriller, a sonically surprising, genre-defying project that marks a softer evolution. “The jaguar exists within me always, but I’m feeling like a softer version of myself now,” she says.
After all, Monét’s critically acclaimed debut studio album Jaguar II was the ultimate learning lesson. “Once you have an accolade like that, people often look for a reason to discredit why you got it,” she explains. “I think—pending a Grammy happens again—I would do the opposite and bunker down in the studio.”
Step inside Monét’s world in our Now Playing Issue, presented by @bvlgari, at the link in our bio.

posts.caption:
“I actually came up with one of the songs on my album while making cookies,” @victoriamonet says. As if the Grammy-winning artist didn’t already have enough on her plate with the upcoming release of her seventh studio album, Monét has also earned herself a culinary school diploma. “It all goes together, food and music,” she says.
Aptly dubbed the “queen of side quests” by followers on social media, Monét is proving that every dream, hobby, and creative pursuit can coexist—even in motherhood. Her biggest wish? To be a living example for her daughter, Hazel. “I’m just trying to show Hazel that she really can do it, even when it’s hard or you don’t get the right amount of sleep or you don’t feel great about your work or whatever it is,” she explains.
Step inside Monét’s world in our Now Playing Issue, presented by @bvlgari, at the link in our bio.

posts.caption:
Pogue for life. @jonathandavissofficial may be looking toward his bright future, but he’ll never forget his OBX family. “Those memories will be there forever, and the people will follow me into the rest of my life. They’ll be at my wedding, and they’ll be there for my kids, and I’ll be there for theirs,” he says.
Now, Daviss is stepping into arguably his biggest project yet: portraying a young Snoop Dogg in the upcoming biopic Snoop. “I didn’t want to do a Snoop Dogg impression,” he explains. “I wanted to make you [the audience] feel like you’re watching Snoop Dogg, a young dude from Long Beach, California, who was in a very specific situation and grew up in a crazy way and did something amazing.”
Read the full interview in our Now Playing Issue, presented by @bvlgari, at the link in our bio.

posts.caption:
@jodiemcomer has never played it safe. ⁠
⁠
After captivating audiences as Villanelle in "Killing Eve," the actress is stepping into quieter, more complex territory with "The Death of Robin Hood." For WWD Weekend's latest cover story, Comer opens up about exploring darker creative spaces, embracing the projects that scare her most and why pushing boundaries still drives her. ⁠
⁠
Read the interview now at the link in bio.⁠
⁠

posts.caption:
Turns out, promoting a comedy is as fun as it looks.⁠
⁠
“What’s nice about this, and I haven’t had so many experiences of this before, is that it’s a really fun show and I’m playing someone playful,” @ellahunt says, sitting barefoot and crossed-legged on a couch. “I don’t have some kind of moral to hammer home or anything, so it feels lighter than I’m used to.”⁠
⁠
Hunt stars in @notsuitableforwork, a new Hulu comedy from Mindy Kaling that follows two groups of roommates, all trying to figure it out in their first jobs in New York City. Hunt plays AJ, a Bostonian who is trying to make it in the world of finance. ⁠
⁠
Read more at WWD.com — #linkinbio⁠
⁠
Report: @leighnordstrom⁠

posts.caption:
Over the last few years @toryburch’s design pivot has brought forth a new signature look — one rooted in empowering personal style with quirky accents, unique color combos, funky fabric innovations and an off-kilter femininity that continues to ooze unfussy cool. ⁠
⁠
For resort, Burch amplified this narrative with an “everything but the kitchen sink” ethos that hit on the resort season’s requirement of offering a little bit of everything for the warmer, colder and everything in-between climates her clients are traveling to. The result was a delightfully optimistic and dressier collection that evoked the modern lady.⁠
⁠
Read more at WWD.com — #linkinbio⁠
⁠

posts.caption:
On Monday night, The @lgbtcenternyc kicked off the first day of Pride Month with its annual Fashion Centered fundraiser.⁠
⁠
The community center, based in the West Village, only closes its doors to host one private event onsite each year. With significant backing from the fashion industry, the Fashion Centered raises support for The Center’s broad resources and programming for LGBTQIA+ New Yorkers. This year’s event, which honored @thombrowne with The NYC LGBT Community Center’s Lifetime Achievement Award, was supported by presenting sponsor Pandora, as well as  Jean Paul Gaultier, Supreme  and Ferragamo.⁠
⁠
Read more at WWD.com — #linkinbio⁠
⁠
Report: @ktauer⁠
Photos: @lexieblacklock/WWD

posts.caption: A very sad day. 💔 This property is iconic in the truest sense of the word. Neiman Marcus defined luxury retail throughout the country.. In its day, “Store 1” at Main and Ervay, was the epicenter of a thriving downtown Dallas. I will always remember my first time at the flagship as an 11 year old out of towner. I was in awe. Fortunate to go to work for the company post grad school, I treasured my every visit each time I had the privilege of being in that beautiful building. And I will cherish my final visit to the legendary specialty store last June when I insisted our group in town on business, lunch in the famous institution known as The Zodiac. Thanks for the memories, NM. Like most break ups, I just never thought it would end this way!

posts.caption:
Where are the “Sephora tweens” now?
More than two years after that term catapulted through the cultural lexicon as Gen Alpha’s first tweens entered the beauty industry — often in droves, with a particular penchant for prestige and in some cases, unsuitable, products — the hype has moderated.
Case in point: NIQ data shows that while facial skin care sales grew 26.7 percent among households with kids ages 13 to 17 in 2023 — the year the “Sephora tween” was coined — that growth has most recently leveled off to about 8.9 percent during the 12 months ending in April.
More meaningful habits, though, have emerged from the frenzy.
Read more details, including which brands and products teen girls are overindexing on today, on WWD.com. #linkinbio
